item.title: Gippsland man charged with threatening federal politician on social media
item.description: A man will face court after federal police charged him following an investigation into a social media post that allegedly threatened a federal politician.

item.title: Community mourns as Mornington Peninsula's Sammy the seal believed dead
item.description: A beloved seal known as Sammy is believed to have died, after Zoos Victoria confirmed they were called to a rescue on Tuesday.

item.title: Man on mobility scooter killed when struck by car on pedestrian crossing
item.description: A 49-year-old man using a mobility scooter has died after being hit by a car on a pedestrian crossing in Alice Springs.

item.title: Farrer's Indigenous and migrant communities worried by rise of One Nation
item.description: David Farley has won Farrer for One Nation in a landslide, but there are some in the community yet to be convinced of his ability to represent them.

item.title: WA man accused of terrorism offences faces child abuse, extremist material charges
item.description: A man accused of planning terrorist attacks in Perth is charged with further offences, including possession of child abuse material and violent extremist material on his devices.

item.title: Here's how federal budget negative gearing and capital gains tax changes will affect you
item.description: Answers to all your questions about the federal budget's changes to negative gearing and the capital gains tax.

item.title: Criminal who used tractor to escape prison farm near Cairns gets more jail time
item.description: The Queensland man's escape triggered a month-long manhunt that ended when he was found at a remote campsite near a rainforest.

item.title: What's in the federal budget for older Australians?
item.description: Despite it being an intergenerational budget, specific parts of the economic strategy will impact older Australians.

item.title: Panthers confident of re-signing Nathan Cleary despite dad's departure
item.description: Penrith Panthers' four-time premiership-winning coach Ivan Cleary announces he will step aside as coach of the NRL club at the end of the 2027 season.

item.title: Patients of retired Sydney dentist urged to test for bloodborne viruses
item.description: Retired dentist William Tam may have exposed his patients to hepatitis B, hepatitis C and HIV at his clinic, according to NSW Health.

item.title: Man, 52, charged with defrauding Commonwealth after police seize suspected military ordnance
item.description: Northern Territory Police have seized 32 explosive ordnance items, including hand grenades, rocket components and warheads in raids south of Darwin.

item.title: High Court sides with TasPorts over 2022 Mersey River tugboats sinking by Goliath
item.description: A shipping company's attempt to avoid paying millions of dollars to Tasmania's port authority over the sinking of two tugboats in Devonport has been overturned by Australia's High Court.

item.title: Free omega-3 testing to be introduced in SA for pregnant women in a bid to prevent preterm birth
item.description: The South Australian government has announced that pregnant women will be able to access free omega-3 blood tests from next month.

item.title: Man charged with drug offences after woman's body found in Joondalup CBD
item.description: Police are investigating after a woman's body was discovered on Grand Boulevard in Joondalup, with a 35-year-old man charged with a string of drug offences.

item.title: Bendigo's Hargreaves Mall could reopen to cars under plan to revitalise area
item.description: A Victorian council is proposing to reopen a regional mall to vehicles after 44 years in an attempt to breathe new life into the area.

item.title: Person shot in stomach at property in Tasmania's north
item.description: Police say it is believed the man in his 30s and the shooter knew each other and were involved in a "disturbance" at the front of the home before the incident.

item.title: Steven May reveals 'mental breakdown' led to abrupt retirement on eve of AFL season
item.description: Steven May opens up on his shock decision to retire from the AFL just two weeks before the season opener for Melbourne.

item.title: Yindjibarndi urged to consider appeal against native title compensation decision
item.description: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to hand over the largest native title compensation payout in Australian history. But some say it falls painfully short of what is owed.

item.title: Parrot stolen from Adelaide pet shop was concealed in woman's top, police say
item.description: South Australian police swoop on the alleged perpetrator of a parrot theft at a suburban Adelaide pet shop, but say the bird has not been found.

item.title: Court grants mining giant Bravus leave to sue council for $22 million over unsealed access road
item.description: A legal stoush between a Central Queensland council and one of Australia's largest coal mines deepens in Queensland's Supreme Court.

item.title: US woman Kouri Richins, who published a book on grief after husband's death, to be sentenced for his murder
item.description: Kouri Richins was convicted of aggravated murder after poisoning her husband with fentanyl and self-publishing a children's book about coping with grief.

item.title: Why Ivan Cleary's decision to leave the Panthers is as unprecedented as their success
item.description: Ivan Cleary is doing what no other coach of his stature has ever done in Australian rugby league history, walking away from the NRL with his team at the height of their powers.

item.title: Labor upgrades net migration numbers by 55,000 in federal budget
item.description: The federal budget reveals the government expects more migrants to arrive to Australia than previously thought, even as it faces a campaign from the Coalition and One Nation to slash migration rates.

item.title: Lawyer for notorious paedophile Peter Liddy says indefinite jail would be an 'abuse of process'
item.description: A lawyer for paedophile former magistrate Peter Liddy asks a court to permanently stay an application to keep his client behind bars, but a lawyer for the SA Attorney-General says Liddy continues to deny his crimes.

item.title: More than 2,000 protesters chant 'stop the chop' to oppose North Adelaide golf project
item.description: More than 2,000 people gathered at Parliament House in Adelaide to protest against the chopping down of trees to make way for the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment.

item.title: Queensland banking on 'unbelievable' Tamika Upton to level State of Origin series
item.description: Queensland needs a win to keep its State of Origin hopes alive and is turning to the inspirational Tamika Upton to provide it.

item.title: NRL's Magic Round to remain in Brisbane until 2032 after government reaches deal for contract extension
item.description: Queensland's deal with the NRL to host Magic Round was originally due to end after 2027, but the state government has reached a deal with the NRL to extend until 2032.

item.title: What's in the 2026 budget for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people?
item.description: First Nations experts have their say on whether the $1.2 billion for Closing the Gap initiatives announced in this year's budget is likely to hit the mark.

item.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Cannon by Lee Lai becomes first graphic novel to win the Stella Prize
item.description: Lee Lai's debut graphic novel, Stone Fruit, was longlisted for the Stella Prize in 2022. Four years on, she's won the prestigious award for her latest book, Cannon.

item.title: Gunshots ring out at Philippine Senate as ICC seeks arrest of former police chief
item.description: Authorities are still trying to work out who fired the gun shots inside the Senate building in the Philippines, prompting people to run for cover.

item.title: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage facing inquiry over $9m gift from crypto billionaire
item.description: Britain's parliamentary watchdog will begin an investigation into whether Reform UK leader Nigel Farage breached its rules by accepting a 5 million pound ($9.31 million) gift that he did not declare.

item.title: It's the ABC More or Less quiz: Eurovision edition
item.description: We've put down our tiny flags and picked up our record books to challenge what you think you might know about Eurovision.

item.title: How Hollywood became a new home for classical music’s grandest ideas
item.description: From 19th-century concert traditions to Hollywood’s Golden Age, and back to the concert hall, movies are helping audiences experience live orchestral music.

item.title: Anne Hathaway and Michaela Coel will bewitch you in Mother Mary
item.description: Director David Lowery has crafted a dark and mysterious chamber piece that turns a pop star's spiralling crisis into something supernatural.

item.title: Economists divided on extent of property price hit from budget tax overhaul
item.description: Some economists are forecasting price falls of up to 5 per cent as a result of measures to reduce intergenerational housing inequality.

item.title: 'From Farage to Farrer': Rising populist tide makes Labor bolder
item.description: If this week's budget marked the start of the Albanese government's campaign for a third term in office, tonight's budget reply marks Angus Taylor's pitch to prevent a Liberal wipe-out.

item.title: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping are meeting in China but only one has the upper hand
item.description: Nine years after his last meeting with Xi Jinping in China, Donald Trump is more aggressive and assertive. But so is Beijing. And its president is ready to take the geopolitical and economic lead.

item.title: Agnes Water accommodation provider fined after refusing to allow woman to stay with guide dog
item.description: A Queensland accommodation provider has been the first to be successful convicted under the state's Guide, Hearing and Assistance Dogs (GHAD) Act.

item.title: Australians 'literally dying' as aged care average wait times surpass 365 days
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has conceded the state of aged care in Australia "is not good enough" after a new report revealed people are waiting an average of a year for services.

item.title: How a sister's love unraveled the tragic death of Olympic hopeful Ian Walker and unmasked the teacher who abused him
item.description: Ian Walker was one of the most promising athletes in the country until his life started to unravel, and the search for the truth in his tragic story has finally come to light.

item.title: TV tower shutdown prompts concern about reliance on foreign satellites
item.description: Phone, internet and council-run TV services have been making way for foreign satellite technology in rural Queensland as ground infrastructure ages, forcing councils to shut down services.

item.title: Autistic teenager 'Thomas Carrick' sentenced over terror-related offences
item.description: A years-long saga that began with a 13-year-old being "radicalised" during a covert police operation is set to escalate to a Federal Court battle.

item.title: Former Australia Test cricketer Marcus North named England selector
item.description: The appointment of its first Australian selector, its first female coach and a new-look team has made it a landmark day for England's men's cricket team as it prepares to plunge into its post-Ashes Test recovery.

item.title: Mixed reaction from Australia's diaspora communities to federal budget's housing reform
item.description: Housing policy changes announced in the federal budget could make it easier for some to buy homes, but others are planning to sell before they lose too much.

item.title: Gun shop owners grapple with uncertainty amid firearm reform
item.description: A lack of clarity on the South Australian government's position on gun control reform has caused a downturn in revenue for retailers.

item.title: Sydney Swans move annual Pride Game away from St Kilda fixture after Lance Collard case
item.description: The fallout continues from the Lance Collard AFL tribunal case, with St Kilda cut from the annual Pride Game against Sydney.

item.title: Michael Voss exit closes door on Carlton's era of premiership contention that never was
item.description: Michael Voss leaves Carlton having taken the club to places it has rarely been in a quarter of a century, but still adrift from where it ultimately wants to be.

item.title: Surfside residents fear homes could be lost to sea in next storm
item.description: Residents living on an idyllic stretch of the NSW far south coast are demanding action to protect their homes from erosion.

item.title: New virus to control surging feral rabbit population at least 10 years away
item.description: Feral rabbits are causing damage on farms and in towns, but biological control research has been delayed by funding woes.

item.title: Teenager charged over home invasion linked to Melbourne arson attacks
item.description: Police say a shot was fired by intruders during an aggravated home invasion earlier this month, which is being linked to a series of arson attacks on licensed venues in Melbourne.

item.title: Drug counsellor Erik Fleming sentenced for role in supplying ketamine to Matthew Perry
item.description: The man who delivered Friends star Matthew Perry the doses of ketamine that killed him says he is haunted by the mistakes he made.

item.title: Public memorial for 702 ABC Sydney presenter James Valentine to be held at Town Hall
item.description: Sydney Town Hall will host a memorial for the late radio presenter and musician, with limited spots available for members of the public.

item.title: Greenbushes residents say Talison's free car wash plan misses the mark
item.description: Residents plagued by dust from Australia's oldest and largest lithium mine say a plan for a free car wash by the mine's owner misses the mark.

item.title: Dennington Dogs forfeit remainder of men's senior Warrnambool District league season
item.description: A country Victorian football team that was beaten by a collective 1,101 points in four matches forfeits the remainder of the senior season.

item.title: Eddie Jones suspended by Japan rugby for verbal abuse on Australian tour
item.description: Eddie Jones has been suspended from coaching Japan during its rugby union Test match against Italy in July after "incidences of verbal abuse" during a recent tour of Australia.

item.title: How will the NT amend child protection laws? Will the Aboriginal Child Placement Principle change?
item.description: Here's what you need to know about the proposed changes to the NT child protection legislation, introduced into parliament on Wednesday.

item.title: Federal Court dismisses legal action over Murray flood plain restoration
item.description: A community group's argument that a delegate to the federal water minister made a legal error by approving the Nyah Floodplain Restoration Project has been rejected.

item.title: Palaszczuk partner Reza Adib's first court hearing for rape charges
item.description: A court hearing has been held for Vahid Reza Adib, the partner of former Queensland premier Annastacia Palaszczuk, for the first time since he was charged with rape, deprivation of liberty and sexual assault earlier this month.

item.title: Coalition to bar non-citizens from welfare, NDIS under fresh migration policy
item.description: Non-citizens, including permanent residents, will be barred from accessing welfare under an updated migration crackdown promised by the Coalition.

item.title: Coles found to have misled shoppers on discounted items in bombshell Federal Court case
item.description: Supermarket giant Coles broke consumer law by misleading shoppers on discount prices, a Federal Court judge has found.

item.title: More than 800 northern corroboree frogs released into the wild in southern NSW to bolster numbers
item.description: More than 800 tiny, technicoloured northern corroboree frogs are released into the wild to try to bolster the numbers of one of Australia's most critically endangered amphibians.

item.title: Scottish Premier League title race goes to final day with Hearts leading Celtic by one point
item.description: Celtic's Kelechi Iheanacho scores a controversial penalty with the last kick of the game to secure a 3-2 win at Motherwell, sending the Scottish Premiership title race to a final-day showdown against leaders Heart of Midlothian.

item.title: How to clean your sneakers in a washing machine
item.description: Experts reveal their tips on bringing your favourite sneakers back to life at home.

item.title: Newcastle Waters cattle station granted permit to cull 20,000 native birds
item.description: A permit to cull 20,000 native birds has been granted to a cattle station in the Northern Territory in response to an "overpopulation" of destructive and "exceptionally noisy" galahs and little corellas.

item.title: Coles has been found to have misled shoppers. These charts show how
item.description: The pricing patterns of these six products show how Coles was found to have misled consumers through its Down Down pricing patterns.

item.title: Why Gold Coast's retro Chargers jersey is already the toast of the NRL's Magic Round
item.description: One of the best parts of Magic Round is going jersey-spotting but before a ball is kicked in Brisbane the Titans have already cleaned up via their Chargers-inspired retro strip.

item.title: Coalition proposes plan to end tax bracket creep
item.description: The Coalition has made a bold play to win back voters with a plan to automatically index income tax brackets to keep up with inflation.

item.title: Police search for driver after hit and run that left boy with 'life-changing' injuries
item.description: Security footage from the Sunshine Coast has been released as the young boy undergoes multiple surgeries to save his leg.

item.title: Gold Coast Suns coach Damien Hardwick says Mark Opie will continue duties despite AFL probe
item.description: Gold Coast Suns coach Damien Hardwick says team manager Mark Opie will continue his match-day duties despite being investigated by the AFL integrity unit over his links to a bookmaking firm.

item.title: Six passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship due to arrive in Perth today
item.description: Six passengers from a cruise ship at the centre of a deadly hantavirus outbreak are testing negative for the illness and are symptom-free, the federal health minister says, as they prepare to be repatriated to Australia.

item.title: 'Pre-existing dispute' likely reason for Wynyard shooting, Tasmania police say
item.description: Police say a shooting yesterday involved people "known to police" and that it "stems from a pre-existing dispute between those two individuals".

item.title: Gold Coast Suns coach Damien Hardwick speaks to media in Darwin
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NDIS bill creates funding cut powers, civil penalties and stricter test for access
item.description: Legislation designed to save $35 billion in NDIS spending is introduced to parliament, revealing new powers for the minister to cut funding to classes of supports, automate some decisions and create requirements for prospective participants to exhaust all other treatment options before entering the NDIS.

item.title: Inquest to be held into disappearance of Queensland man Nathan Brosnan
item.description: Nathan Brosnan, 35, went missing from Logan while on parole. He is suspected to have died, but his body has never been found.

item.title: Homicide squad investigating suspicious death in Katanning
item.description: The man was found unresponsive on a suburban street in Katanning early yesterday morning.

item.title: NSW Ambulance experts quizzed at Clare Nowland taser-death inquest
item.description: A senior NSW Ambulance officer tells the Clare Nowland inquest that emergency services could have taken more time responding to the 95-year-old's dementia-related aggression.

item.title: Korean researcher flown out of Antarctica after knife incident at Jang Bogo Station
item.description: A member of an Antarctic expedition who allegedly threatened colleagues with a bladed weapon has been removed from a Korean research station in an emergency evacuation.

item.title: Roads of dead mice as plague blights standards of living in WA towns
item.description: As locals in a Western Australian town battle a siege of mice, farmers still do not have permission to use a high-strength bait to combat them.

item.title: 3D-printed guns, taser and ammunition among weapons cache seized in Canberra
item.description: A police investigation has uncovered a large cache of weapons in the national capital's suburbs, less than a kilometre from the Belconnen Police Station.

item.title: Police mine shaft search fails to find evidence of missing SA woman Trisha Graf
item.description: Major Crime investigators have packed up after focusing on outback SA mine shafts in their search for 41-year-old Trisha Graf, who went missing last year.

item.title: Madonna, Shakira and BTS to co-headline World Cup final half-time show
item.description: The first-ever FIFA World Cup final half-time show has been announced.

item.title: Albanese pulls Tammy Tyrrell trick, battlelines drawn in tax fight
item.description: After failing to catch anyone by surprise with the budget, Anthony Albanese had another trick up his sleeve.

item.title: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping's meeting in China featured tech titans, Taiwan and flattery
item.description: From a red carpet welcome to a high-profile tech entourage and a pointed reference to Taiwan, here are the main takeaways from Donald Trump's meeting with Xi Jinping in China.

item.title: Who is in President Trump's entourage of billionaires and executives in Beijing?
item.description: US President Donald Trump has brought a delegation of business executives for his visit to Beijing. Here's why they made the trans-Pacific trip.

item.title: Illawarra grandmother blames horrific roadside stabbing on Ozempic
item.description: An Illawarra woman stabbed multiple times by her mother-in-law has told a court she continues to be haunted by images of the roadside attack.

item.title: Bosisto's to triple its eucalyptus oil production with new $10 million distillery in central Victoria
item.description: A 174-year-old Australian company is trying to address a decline in local eucalyptus oil production by opening a new distillery in central Victoria.

item.title: Coalition wants to slash migration, but businesses say economy needs skills
item.description: The Coalition wants to limit Australia's net migration intake to only one person per new home built, but businesses say the economy needs a "sustainable" migration program.

item.title: One Nation wedged, Greens steamrolled in SA Labor's two-front environmental battle
item.description: The Malinauskas government's push to end the fracking moratorium in South East SA is a brutal welcome to politics for a new One Nation MP, as Labor fights an environmental war against both the left and right.

item.title: Calls for more education following the suspected death of Mornington Peninsula's beloved 'Sammy' the seal
item.description: Marine experts are calling for greater education on aquatic life following the death of a seal believed to be known by Mornington Peninsula locals as Sammy.

item.title: Miraculous try-savers hand NSW second straight Origin series win
item.description: For the second straight year, NSW secures the Women's State of Origin shield in Game II, with Jesse Southwell and Teagan Berry coming up with stunning try-savers to get the Blues over the line.

item.title: Mannagh's heavenly night leads Cats to statement win over Lions
item.description: Geelong records a 41-point win over Brisbane on the back of Shaun Mannagh's five-goal, 30-disposal performance at the Gabba.

item.title: Angus Taylor denies immigrants will 'be forced' to give up anything after budget in reply speech
item.description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has defended his comments aimed at non-citizens as necessary and practical during a heated interview on 7.30.

item.title: Leadership hopefuls Wes Streeting and Andy Burnham edge towards challenging UK PM Keir Starmer
item.description: The UK's health secretary has resigned from cabinet and another Labour MP has quit parliament paving the way for a potential leadership challenge to Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

item.title: ICC fugitive Ronald dela Rosa's whereabouts unknown after fleeing Philippine Senate
item.description: Senator Ronald dela Rosa, who is wanted by the International Criminal Court on charges of crimes against humanity, had been holed up inside the Philippine Senate since Monday to avoid arrest. His whereabouts is now unknown.

item.title: Latvian PM Evika Silina resigns in row over stray Ukrainian drones
item.description: Drones launched by Ukraine that were jammed by Russia either flew over or crashed in the Baltic country's territory, with local residents critical of the government's response.

item.title: Seven dead, scores missing in Kyiv after Russian drone hits apartment building
item.description: Russia launches hundreds of drones at Ukraine in the third straight day of widespread attacks on targets across the country.

item.title: Chinese President Xi Jinping hosts state banquet for US President Donald Trump
item.description: Guests dined on Beijing roast duck and tiramisu while the People's Liberation Army band performed some classic hits.

item.title: Sam Kerr to leave Chelsea Football Club after six years
item.description: The Matildas star has spent over six years playing for the London-based team.

item.title: Sensory-friendly show gives theatre lovers a more inclusive experience
item.description: The State Theatre Company South Australia has introduced a sensory-friendly performance for a broader range of audience members including neurodivergent people.

item.title: Taylor's bold budget reply a mix of sound and dubious policy commitments
item.description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor commits to phasing in income tax indexation under a "tax back guarantee" that he says "will fully protect all taxpayers from inflation".

item.title: Coalition will repeal Labor's property tax changes and spend $22b on income tax cuts
item.description: The Coalition would repeal all of Labor's negative gearing and capital gains changes and spend $22.5 billion over four years handing back tax "bracket creep" to workers if elected.

item.title: Labor and the Liberals have staked their fortunes on duelling visions for income taxes
item.description: Labor and the Liberals are offering imperfect but undeniably bold alternative visions for the tax system. It's nice to have something to talk about.

item.title: Ambulance Victoria data shows 59 overdose call-outs at Crown Melbourne in two years
item.description: Paramedics have attended more than 50 suspected overdoses at Crown Melbourne in the past two years, data released exclusively to the ABC shows.

item.title: How the wisdom of experience won the State of Origin series for New South Wales
item.description: New South Wales series win over Queensland proves that sometimes, like anything else, practice makes perfect in State of Origin football.

item.title: TasInsure unravelling a lesson for Tasmanian Liberals in promise management
item.description: In a week where "broken promise" is being shouted by the Tasmanian Liberals in support of their federal colleagues' attack on the Labor budget, closer to home, a promise of their own is giving them grief.

item.title: High-income earners use discretionary trusts to pay less tax, but will budget change have unintended consequences?
item.description: The federal budget includes a new 30 per cent minimum tax on discretionary trusts. Experts say it is not just the ultra-wealthy that will be hit.

item.title: Modernising Queensland veterinary act essential for future of industry, experts say
item.description: Experts says veterinary technologists and nurses are being under-utilised in a sector they say is in "crisis."

item.title: Tens of thousands of rugby league fans descend on Brisbane's Lang Park for blockbuster Magic Round
item.description: Brisbane's Lang Park plays host to the NRL's biggest weekend, with 16 teams competing in eight matches, plus game two of the NRLW State of Origin.

item.title: Chinese Australians lash Coalition proposal to block non-citizens from welfare services
item.description: Chinese Australians have lashed the Coalition's plan to block non-citizens from accessing welfare services, likening the opposition's targeting of migrants to One Nation.

item.title: Twenty years since wrong Guy resulted in greatest mix-up on live TV
item.description: Guy Gomo reflects on his infamous live TV mix-up, which has been immortalised as one of the greatest TV blunders of all time.

item.title: Magic Round crowds told to pack raincoats ahead of patchy Queensland showers
item.description: A trough in the west and onshore winds in the east will bring showers to parts of Queensland, however the driest parts are set to miss out.

item.title: Liberal Party mistakenly emails branch about 'deceased member'
item.description: When John Ternel quit the Victorian Liberal Party this week the party mistakenly sent an email to members saying he had died. Mr Ternel says the ordeal confirmed to him that the Victorian Liberals are in shambles.

item.title: ABC weekly news quiz: A Eurovision boycott, a pungent frog, a mysterious red globule and some impressive Neanderthals
item.description: This is not the week to boycott the news quiz, when a particularly ripe frog and a big, red … something … await you.

item.title: Australian government rejects clean-up deadline for Antarctica's Wilkes Station
item.description: The Australian government has officially ruled out cleaning up Antarctica's contaminated Wilkes Station before the end of the decade, as previously recommended by a federal parliamentary committee.

item.title: How Palantir Helps Agencies “Connect the Dots”
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Energy Australia's bid to build 1.4-gigawatt gas-fired power plant at Marulan
item.description: Energy Australia says the proposed 1.4-gigawatt gas-fired power plant would be critical to the energy transition by supplying reliable back-up power, but analysts have flagged supply and economic issues.

item.title: South East SA locals voice outrage at proposal to lift fracking ban
item.description: Producers on South Australia's Limestone Coast say they are stunned by a government proposal to lift the fracking ban in the region.

item.title: Residents feel impact of no permanent GP in Strahan since 2017
item.description: Jamie hopes his small town's almost decade-long run without a permanent GP is about to end, so he will not have to keep repeating his story to every doctor who passes through.

item.title: Archaeologists restore colonial-era boat found in Barangaroo
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Archaeologists work to restore colonial-era boat found during construction of Barangaroo metro
item.description: Maritime archaeologists are working to restore the oldest colonial-era boat ever found in Australia, discovered during the construction of the Barangaroo metro station in 2018.

item.title: Jensen Plowright avoids late crash to finish fourth on Giro d'Italia stage six, Davide Ballerini claims win
item.description: Jensen Plowright never expected to be contesting for stage victories at this year's Giro d'Italia. But the withdrawal of Kaden Groves and yet another calamitous crash gave him his first chance on stage six.

item.title: Seismic testing linked by research to decreased fish catch numbers in Gippsland
item.description: New research links seismic testing used for oil and gas exploration with decreases in commercial fish catch rates in Victoria's east.

item.title: Federal budget commits $50m to duplicate Canberra road amid warnings it could increase peak-hour traffic
item.description: The latest federal budget has committed $50 million to duplicate a road in West Belconnen, but transport planning experts warn it could encourage even more peak-hour traffic.

item.title: Music program takes regional artists from the Northern Territory to UK for The Great Escape festival
item.description: Northern Territory musicians attending The Great Escape music festival in the UK are hoping their experience will open more doors for regional artists.

item.title: Delta Goodrem makes Eurovision 2026 grand final after performing pop ballad Eclipse
item.description: Delta Goodrem will perform in the Eurovision 2026 grand final for Australia after a rousing performance of the song Eclipse during the semifinal.

item.title: Government lists neo-Nazi group as prohibited hate group in Australia — as it happened
item.description: Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke announces the organisation colloquially known as neo-Nazis has been listed as a prohibited hate group.

item.title: Delta Goodrem performs at Eurovision semifinals
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Champions League race overshadowed as Serie A serves up fixture chaos thanks to Italian Open
item.description: The race for the Champions League spots has been overshadowed as Serie A serves up fixture chaos relating to the Italian Open tennis tournament in Rome.

item.title: Mount Isa homes to be sold after Indigenous organisation fails to pay rates
item.description: Tenants in some Mount Isa homes have endured years without repairs, hot water or proper leases and now one corporation's failures could leave them homeless.

item.title: ASX closes lower, oil price up as Trump and Xi discuss war in Iran — as it happened
item.description: The ASX200 finished the week lower, and oil headed for a weekly gain as the reopening of the critical Strait of Hormuz waterway and end to the war in the Middle East remain in doubt.

item.title: Seals found entangled in fishing line or rope on the rise
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Wildlife rescuers say more fur seals are becoming entangled in fishing gear
item.description: Data from the wildlife rescue group ORRCA shows an increase in the number of fur seal entanglements as they seek out fishing lines in attempts to find an easy meal.

item.title: Min Woo Lee takes share of clubhouse lead at PGA Championship, Scotty Scheffler ominously poised
item.description: Australian Min Woo Lee enjoys a share of the first-round clubhouse lead, but Masters champ Rory McIlroy has endured a rough start to the PGA Championship.

item.title: US vote to halt Iran war narrowly defeated as Israel and Lebanon have 'productive' first day of talks
item.description: Israel and Lebanon have reported a productive first day of talks in Washington as the deadline for the ceasefire between the two countries approaches.

item.title: Man faces court charged with murdering elderly mother in her Canberra home
item.description: Marcus Kauffman made a brief appearance in the ACT Magistrates Court this morning, where no bail application was made.

item.title: WA Premier Roger Cook backs federal budget housing focus
item.description: Roger Cook welcomes the federal budget's focus on housing, but he's less than thrilled about NDIS changes and the funding of infrastructure and aged care.

item.title: Surfers conquer huge Kimberley tidal rapid in Western Australia's north
item.description: Pro surfers Anthony Walsh and Dylan Graves have conquered the never-before-surfed Kimberley tidal rapid in Western Australia's remote north.

item.title: Melanie Lynskey pays tribute to the women driving the Pike River Mine disaster campaign
item.description: Standing side by side with real-life heroes Anna Osborne and Sonya Rockhouse, the Yellow Jackets star is a passionate ally for their cause.

item.title: Budget designed to help young people criticised as still favouring baby boomers
item.description: The prime minister says this budget is about fixing intergenerational inequality, but online, gen Z and the millennials are hearing a different narrative.

item.title: Activist, 78, arrested at North Adelaide Golf Course, as protesters chain themselves to machinery
item.description: An elderly activist has been arrested after breaking through the gates of the North Adelaide Golf Course, while protesters earlier this morning claimed they had chained themselves to machinery in condemnation of hundreds of trees being cut.

item.title: Victorian country firefighters slam bureaucracy for slashing roadside burns by 90pc
item.description: Victorian fire authorities release new findings that show how planned burns helped control and contain the devastating summer bushfires.

item.title: Police charge teens over arson attempt at Bar Bambi in Melbourne
item.description: Police allege Bar Bambi in ACDC Lane has again been targeted by arsonists, while officers also investigate suspicious truck fires at two businesses in Melbourne's inner-west.

item.title: Vaccine program almost halves number of young babies hospitalised with RSV
item.description: A new immunisation program for pregnant mothers and newborns has almost halved the number of young babies being hospitalised with a serious respiratory virus, according to a new report.

item.title: Manjimup's migrant community unite to mourn deaths of Vanuatu farm workers in horror crash
item.description: Residents of a WA country town have come together to mourn the deaths of two Pacific island farm workers as investigations into their deaths continue.

item.title: Australia vs Ireland AFLW clash locked in for North Sydney Oval in August
item.description: Australia and Ireland will meet in an international women's match under Australian rules to kick off the 2026 AFLW season.

item.title: National Commissioner for Indigenous children says people chosen to lead NT child protection review won't 'cut it'
item.description: The National Commissioner for Indigenous children has criticised the selection of a retired NSW police commissioner and long-standing NT public servant as the leaders of a review into the territory's child protection system.

item.title: 'We want the straits open': Trump says China agrees on ending the Iran war — as it happened
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping praised the building of a "stable relationship" with the US as the two world leaders met in Beijing. Take a look back at the historic state visit. Take a look back at how the day unfolded.

item.title: Matthew Wale voted in as new Solomon Islands prime minister after secret ballot
item.description: Matthew Wale is the new prime minister of Solomon Islands after winning a vote in the Pacific island nation's parliament.

item.title: Angus Taylor's budget reply shows times have changed since Peter Dutton
item.description: To appreciate how much times have changed, it is worth comparing the opposition leader's budget reply speech to Peter Dutton's.

item.title: Israeli nationalists march through Jerusalem Old City to mark annexation of East Jerusalem
item.description: Thousands of Israeli nationalists, including young boys, join crowds chanting death threats towards Palestinians while marching through Jerusalem Old City.

item.title: Northern Territory records Australia's first diphtheria death reported in almost a decade amid outbreak
item.description: The Northern Territory has recorded the country's first reported death from diphtheria in almost a decade, as experts urge people to get vaccinated.

item.title: Calls for drought declaration as southern Queensland graziers begin to destock
item.description: Graziers start destocking to preserve dwindling feed with rainfall well below average across the region.

item.title: Culture, gender and birth order meant I would always be in the 'sandwich generation'
item.description: As the eldest daughter in a Vietnamese family, it was an unspoken expectation I would be the one to care for my aging parents.

item.title: Cocaine hidden in fruit pulp found by Australian Federal Police at Melbourne Airport
item.description: Police say criminal syndicates are becoming more creative at concealing drugs within airport shipments.

item.title: What we know so far about Trump's trip to China
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Federal Court rejects northern cattle industry's appeal over impact of live export ban
item.description: Australia's northern cattle industry says it is "deeply disappointed" by a Federal Court decision to dismiss its appeal over compensation for the 2011 live export ban.

item.title: Carlton interim coach Josh Fraser not ready to take full-time role after Michael Voss exit
item.description: Josh Fraser will lead Carlton for the remainder of the season after Michael Voss's exit, but isn't prepared to do the job full-time just yet.

item.title: NT Police arrest man in Alice Springs with swastika tattooed on his leg following five-month investigation
item.description: A 25-year-old man has been arrested and charged in Alice Springs for displaying a tattoo of a swastika on his leg.

item.title: Sydney doctor Andrew Small charged with alleged sexual assault of patients
item.description: Sydney GP Andrew Small is granted bail, after being charged with allegedly sexually assaulting multiple female patients at an inner-city medical centre.

item.title: The federal government wants to use AI to speed up drug and housing approvals. Can we trust it?
item.description: This week’s budget revealed a number of ways the federal government is expected to use artificial intelligence to cut back on red tape. One expert says the benefits are "mostly good".

item.title: Flight with Australian and New Zealand passengers from hantavirus cruise ship arrives in Perth
item.description: A flight carrying six people who were on board the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship lands at an air force base in Perth.

item.title: Eight people arrested at North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment site, police say
item.description: Police say eight people have been arrested at the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment site this morning.

item.title: Push to overturn South East fracking ban to be blocked by One Nation, Liberals
item.description: A controversial plan to end a ban on fracking in South Australia's South East two years earlier than planned, appears to be all but dead, with the Liberals and One Nation vowing to oppose legislation.

item.title: Sydney and Fremantle lead early flag chase, but dual styles make Hawthorn a strong chance
item.description: An early review of the AFL season so far points to a split between two distinct styles, with one side making a case for being flag favourites by showing it can deliver both.

item.title: Liberty Bell Bay smelter workers get another two weeks pay reprieve
item.description: Workers at Australia's only manganese smelter will be thrown an additional lifeline as the state and federal governments step in with a two-week pay offer. It comes days before a previous short-term deal was due to expire.

item.title: Court upholds discrimination ruling on appeal after transgender woman excluded from Giggle for Girls app
item.description: ​A landmark Federal Court ruling that the exclusion of a transgender woman from a female-only app constituted unlawful gender identity discrimination is upheld on appeal.

item.title: Xi made a historical reference as a warning to Trump. What is the Thucydides Trap?
item.description: The US and China have been rivals for almost 80 years, but when the countries' leaders met in Beijing on Thursday, Xi Jinping made reference to a far more ancient feud.

item.title: An elderly activist was among eight people arrested at the North Adelaide Golf Course redevelopment site.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Four students in hospital after suspected drug 'overdose' at Central Coast high school
item.description: Fifteen students at a Central Coast high school have been assessed by paramedics, with four being taken to hospital.

item.title: ABC boss Hugh Marks flags TV program cuts as broadcaster pivots to new platforms
item.description: The ABC's managing director says the public broadcaster's biggest challenge is "stopping things" as he seeks to grow audiences on new platforms without increasing staff.

item.title: How to kill off your lawn and plant a native garden instead
item.description: Many of us are looking to create a native garden as a lower maintenance, more sustainable and often cheaper alternative.

item.title: Coalition tax plan could cost more than suggested in first few years
item.description: The Coalition's plan to index tax brackets could cost $35 billion in the first four years, more than has been suggested, and $44 billion in one year alone in 2035-36.

item.title: Former Victorian premier Daniel Andrews to be immortalised with statue
item.description: The government says work is already underway to honour the state's longest-serving Labor premier.

item.title: Could Delta Goodrem win Eurovision for Australia? The odds have soared in her favour
item.description: In a dazzling show of light and a powerful, tone-perfect delivery, Delta Goodrem may have just given Australia a real shot at winning Eurovision for the first time.

item.title: Socceroos star Mo Toure says he went with a hunch by moving to Norwich City
item.description: Rising Socceroos striker Mo Toure says a "gut feeling" was the reason behind his wildly successful move to the English Championship.

item.title: Donald Trump and Xi Jinping meet at Zhongnanhai Garden
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Speeding driver who struck and killed novelist Brenda Walker jailed for four years
item.description: Lucas Martins Da Silva was driving his hatchback more than 30 kilometres an hour over the speed limit when he struck Brenda Walker in Perth's CBD.

item.title: Universities warn against Coalition plan to cut foreign student numbers in migration crackdown
item.description: Universities are bracing for foreign student numbers to absorb the bulk of cuts under a Coalition migration plan, with experts warning skilled visas would be difficult to cull.

item.title: More than 20 courthouses across regional WA set to close due to lack of security
item.description: Western Australia's top judges have released a public notice saying many courthouses in regional and remote areas will close for at least three months due to a lack of security.

item.title: Man charged with attempted murder over Wynyard shooting of 37-year-old man
item.description: The man allegedly responsible for a shooting in Tasmania's north west on Wednesday has been charged with attempted murder.

item.title: Education union reaches in-principle agreement that will give Victorian teachers pay rises up to 32 per cent
item.description: A drawn out pay dispute between Victoria's government and public sector teachers is set to come to an end, with the union reaching an in-principle deal for pay rises between 28 and 32 per cent.

item.title: NSW coroner acknowledges "powerful presence" of Clare Nowland's family at inquest
item.description: Family of Clare Nowland have addressed a coroners court on the final day of an inquest examining her death.

item.title: Children to be held in police watch houses for up to 48 hours under NT youth justice changes
item.description: Northern Territory Police will be able to hold children in watch houses for up to 48 hours and question them without an adult present under planned changes to the Youth Justice Act.

item.title: Islamophobia envoy speaks out after government fails to respond to landmark report
item.description: The author of a landmark report on Islamophobia in Australia says the federal government is taking too long to act on the issue.

item.title: Massage therapist sentenced to more than 13 years' jail for sexual abuse of 61 victims
item.description: Two women who were sexually assaulted by an unqualified massage therapist have implored women to not feel afraid to "speak up" if they're ever in the same situation, after he was sentenced to more than 13 years in prison.

item.title: Charlie Pickering to host 774 ABC Melbourne Drive from June as Ali Moore steps down
item.description: Veteran broadcaster Ali Moore will step away from the show at the end of the month, with Charlie Pickering to join the program as the new host.

item.title: Donald Trump boards Air Force One after Beijing trip
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Neo-Nazi group banned in Australia under hate laws
item.description: White Australia will be listed as a hate group, making it illegal to support the organisation in any way.

item.title: Bennett insists Latrell will be fit for Origin despite injury exit in loss to Dolphins
item.description: South Sydney coach Wayne Bennett says he expects Latrell Mitchell to take his place in State of Origin I despite the star centre hobbling off late in the Rabbitohs' Magic Round clash with the Dolphins.

item.title: Chloroform suspected as reason Launceston General Hospital ward shut, staff ill, documents reveal
item.description: An incident that prompted a Tasmanian hospital theatre to evacuate, and left several staff feeling ill, was possibly caused by chloroform, documents reveal.

item.title: Police crack down on Shepparton shoplifters and retail crime with dedicated patrols
item.description: In the regional Victorian town of Shepparton, shoplifting and retail crime are so rife that police have launched a dedicated patrol unit to target repeat offenders.

item.title: Costs under 'pressure' for controversial Perth Park racetrack project, state treasurer says
item.description: Despite assurances to the contrary, WA Treasurer Rita Saffioti has conceded it would be difficult for the government to stick to the $217.5 million budget for the Perth Park project.

item.title: Community speakers unanimous in opposition to Tarago waste-to-energy plan at Goulburn hearing
item.description: A parliamentary hearing into a proposed $600 million waste-to-energy plant at Tarago has heard widespread opposition and condemnation from the community.

item.title: Friday finance with Dan Ziffer
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Invasive 'barbecue stopper' Asian tiger mosquito detected away from entry ports on mainland for first time
item.description: The world's most invasive mosquito has previously been found at borders, islands, and ports, but has just been discovered in a remote mainland Queensland community.

item.title: Developer David Trask bids $50,000 donation for a lunch with Queensland Deputy Premier Jarrod Bleijie
item.description: The developer, David Trask, made the successful bid as a donation at lunch on Friday for the ruling LNP, held at the Brisbane Convention Centre and dubbed "Delivering for Queensland Industry Update".

item.title: Swans hold on for gritty victory over Pies, Suns see off Port
item.description: The ladder-leading Sydney Swans scrape past an undermanned but plucky Collingwood side, as Gold Coast defeats Port Adelaide in Darwin.

item.title: Five divers die following cave diving incident
item.description: The group was exploring a series of underwater caves at a depth of around 50 metres in Vaavu Atoll in the Maldives.

item.title: Auckland FC cruises past Adelaide United to reach maiden A-League Men's grand final
item.description: Auckland becomes the first team from New Zealand to qualify for an A-League Men's season decider after making light work of Adelaide at Hindmarsh Stadium.
